description: Senior Python backend dev applying for senior backend role — should pass all criteria
dealbreakers: no Python or Go experience; no backend API development
criteria: Experience designing REST or gRPC APIs
Worked with distributed systems or microservices
Experience with PostgreSQL or another relational database
candidate_summary:
João Silva. Backend engineer focused on Python and distributed systems. LinkedIn: Senior Backend Engineer @ Stripe. Career history:
- Senior Backend Engineer at Stripe (2021-03–present): Designed gRPC and REST payment APIs in Python/Go handling 2M+ TPS. Led migration from monolith to microservices across 6 services.
- Backend Engineer at N26 (2018-07–2021-02): Built Python FastAPI services for banking platform. Owned PostgreSQL schema design and query optimisation for 5M user accounts.
- Junior Python Developer at Freelance (2016-01–2018-06): Built Django REST backends for e-commerce clients. GitHub: 620 commits, 14 OSS repos. Years of experience: 9.2. Seniority (LinkedIn): senior. Career trajectory: ascending. Location: Berlin, Germany.

description: React/Vue frontend dev applying for backend role — should hit dealbreaker
dealbreakers: no Python or Go experience; no backend API development
criteria: Experience designing REST or gRPC APIs
Worked with distributed systems or microservices
Experience with PostgreSQL or another relational database
candidate_summary:
Anna Müller. Frontend engineer. LinkedIn: Senior Frontend Engineer @ Zalando. Career history:
- Senior Frontend Engineer at Zalando (2020-04–present): Built React component library used by 30 squads. Led migration from Redux to Zustand. Performance optimisation reduced bundle size by 35%.
- Frontend Developer at Delivery Hero (2017-09–2020-03): Vue.js SPA development. Worked with REST APIs built by backend team but did not author any backend code.
- UI Developer at Agency (2015-01–2017-08): HTML/CSS/JavaScript, jQuery. Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, CSS. GitHub: 310 commits, 5 OSS repos. Years of experience: 10.1. Seniority (LinkedIn): senior. Career trajectory: ascending. Location: Berlin, Germany.

description: ML engineer with production experience applying for ML role — all criteria should pass
dealbreakers: no Python; no experience training ML models; no production ML deployment
criteria: Proficiency in Python and PyTorch or TensorFlow
Experience training and evaluating large models
Familiarity with data pipelines and feature engineering
candidate_summary:
Lena Fischer. ML engineer specialising in NLP and recommendation systems. LinkedIn: Staff ML Engineer @ Spotify. Career history:
- Staff ML Engineer at Spotify (2022-01–present): Led training of transformer-based playlist recommendation model (500M params, PyTorch). Deployed to production serving 400M users via TorchServe. Reduced P99 latency by 30%.
- Senior ML Engineer at Klarna (2019-06–2021-12): Built fraud detection models (XGBoost, LightGBM). Owned feature store pipeline (Feast + Spark). Model served 1M daily transactions.
- ML Engineer at Research Lab (2017-03–2019-05): TensorFlow model training for image classification. Wrote data preprocessing pipelines. Languages: Python, Scala. GitHub: 480 commits, 9 OSS repos. Years of experience: 8.5. Seniority (LinkedIn): staff. Career trajectory: ascending. Location: Stockholm, Sweden.

criteria: Proficiency in Python and PyTorch or TensorFlow

Marco Rossi. Data analyst focused on business intelligence and reporting. LinkedIn: Senior Data Analyst @ MediaCom. Career history:
- Senior Data Analyst at MediaCom (2020-03–present): Built Tableau dashboards for marketing KPIs. SQL query optimisation across BigQuery. Coordinated with data engineering team for ETL pipeline scheduling.
- Data Analyst at Publicis (2017-10–2020-02): Excel/PowerBI reporting, Google Analytics. No programming or ML work. Languages: SQL. GitHub: 22 commits, 0 OSS repos. Years of experience: 8.0. Seniority (LinkedIn): mid. Career trajectory: lateral. Location: Milan, Italy.

description: 2-year backend dev applying for senior role — should be partial on experience, not disqualified on stack
